messages.content: Hi. Translate this to russian "all the experience i had had had had no effect on my life"
Все опыты, которые я имел, никак не повлияли на мою жизнь.
Hi. Translate this to russian “all the experience i had had no effect on my life”
Весь опыт, который я имел, не имел никакого влияния на мою жизнь.

The difference between the two translations lies in the use of the word "had" in the original sentence and its placement. In the first translation, "all the experience I had had had no effect on my life," the word "had" is used three times consecutively to convey the past perfect tense and emphasize the cumulative nature of the experiences. However, this repetition can sound somewhat confusing and awkward in English.

In the second translation, "all the experience I had had no effect on my life," the word "had" is used only twice, conveying the past tense without the repetition. This version sounds more natural and fluid in Russian. While the meaning remains the same in both translations, the second one is typically preferred due to its smoother and clearer phrasing.
